changesProposed.name: Firehawk Aerospace

verified.name: Firehawk Aerospace
verified.description: Developer of 3D-printed solid rocket motors, base bleed motors, and hybrid rocket engines for defense applications using additive manufacturing technology.
verified.notes: Database entry lists Addison, TX as location; however, sources [0] and [1] specify Lawton, Oklahoma facility as the primary manufacturing location. Founder name, founding year, funding stage, and investor details from database entry could not be verified from provided sources. Source [2] is unrelated to Firehawk Aerospace.

changesProposed.name: Humanoid

verified.name: Humanoid
verified.description: Humanoid develops industrial humanoid robots, including the HMND 01 Alpha Wheeled model, equipped with proprietary AI framework KinetIQ for autonomous task execution in warehouses, logistics, and manufacturing.
verified.notes: Database entry stated $50M founder-led capital and Seed stage, but sources [3] and [5] confirm $152M Series A at $1.35B valuation. Founded year 2024 from database could not be verified in sources. Founder Artem Sokolov confirmed as team member with data engineering background; source [1] lists him but does not explicitly state 'founder' title, though database identifies him as such.

changesProposed.name: SpaceX

verified.name: SpaceX
verified.description: American spaceflight, telecommunications, and artificial intelligence company headquartered in Starbase, Texas. Operates three divisions: Space (orbital launches), Connectivity (Starlink satellite communications), and Artificial intelligence (Grok and data centers).
verified.notes: SpaceX completed its initial public offering on June 12, 2026, raising $86 billion, described as the largest IPO in history. Wikipedia entry indicates Elon Musk owns 42% of outstanding shares and controls 85% of voting power via super-voting stock. Database entry references $1.5T valuation and IPO status, but Wikipedia does not provide a current post-IPO valuation figure, only historical IPO details.

changesProposed.name: Ursa Major Technologies

verified.name: Ursa Major Technologies
verified.description: American aerospace company that produces rocket engines and propulsion systems for space launch, hypersonics, and defense applications. The company manufactures liquid oxygen/kerosene engines and solid rocket motors using additive manufacturing and modular design.
verified.notes: Sources confirm Space Angels Network participation in 2017 Series A ($8M) and BlackRock leading Series C in December 2021 ($85M). Database entry lists 'Series E' and '$400M+' but no sources verify these claims. Wikipedia indicates company is 'Private' with no current acquisition or IPO status indicated.
